item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → Check for staleness first:
   - Read `build_start_utc` and `rounds` from the status file
   - If `rounds` is empty AND more than 30 minutes have elapsed since `build_start_utc`, the master job stalled mid-scaffold. **Treat this as `"waiting_continuation"`** — fall through to step 5 and take over the build yourself.
   - Otherwise, a build agent is genuinely running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

---
name: iwsdk-dev
description: >-
  MUST read BEFORE building VR/MR or browser-first 3D apps with IWSDK (Immersive
  Web SDK / @iwsdk/core). A phase-based build guide — scaffold → world+loop →
  subsystems → spatial UI → verify → deploy → iterate — that keeps the
  non-negotiable correctness rules always in view and loads a focused reference
  per phase. Use when scaffolding, building, debugging, or deploying WebXR/3D
  apps, when working with PanelUI/uikitml, ECS systems, physics, the iwsdk CLI,
  or when the user mentions iwsdk, IWSDK, @iwsdk, WebXR, or `npx iwsdk`.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

Targets the IWSDK **0.4.x** line · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · built on Three.js (super-three) + an ECS.

This skill is phase-based. Find the phase you're in below, read its terse highlights here,
and **load the one matching `references/` file** for depth. The correctness rules in the next
section are always in force, in every phase.

## CLI-first operating model

`npx iwsdk ...` is the primary control surface in cloud/headless harnesses — for setup, dev
server, screenshots, ECS/XR inspection, and **reference lookup**. Prefer it over MCP tools,
which are often unwired in cloud harnesses; do not rewrite CLI examples into MCP-only flows.

**Ground your API knowledge in the reference CLI instead of guessing.** `@iwsdk/reference` is a
semantic search over the real SDK (`reference search` / `api` / `systems` / `components` /
`examples`). It needs a **one-time warmup** that downloads a corpus + model into cache:

```bash
npx iwsdk reference warmup     # one-time, ~120MB into cache; run it during scaffold (Phase 0)
npx iwsdk reference status      # confirm ready
npx iwsdk reference api    --input-json '{"name":"World.create"}'
npx iwsdk reference search --input-json '{"query":"how to register a per-frame system","limit":5}'
```

> **If a reference command errors as not-ready / cache-miss / corpus-missing, RUN
> `npx iwsdk reference warmup` and retry — do NOT give up and guess the API.** Warmup is cheap
> and pays for itself: it is your ground truth for any "what's the real method/component?"
> question and the antidote to the `(world as any)` / wrong-API guessing that breaks builds.
> (See `references/cli-reference.md` for warmup recovery, and `references/api-quick-reference.md`
> for the typed surface at a glance.)

## The 7 build phases

| Phase                       | You are…                                     | Load                                   |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 0 · Scaffold & First Render | creating the app + getting a frame to render | `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md` |
| 1 · World & the Game Loop   | calling `World.create`, standing up the loop | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`      |
| 2 · Gameplay Subsystems     | three imports, physics, audio, input, coords | `references/03-subsystems.md`          |
| 3 · Spatial UI (PanelUI)    | authoring + wiring uikitml panels            | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`          |
| 4 · Verify (continuous)     | proving it renders, ticks, responds          | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`      |
| 5 · Build & Deploy          | deploying — Hatch Space (default), GH Pages opt-in | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`    |
| 6 · Iterate                 | re-verifying + extending across rounds       | `references/07-iterate.md`             |

## Non-negotiable correctness rules (always in force)

These six prevent the most expensive failures. Each is _decided early but surfaces late_, so
internalize them up front. Terse here; full treatment in the tagged reference.

1. **Import every Three.js class from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`.** The runtime is
   super-three; importing from `three` (or `import * as THREE`) drifts types and `instanceof`.
   IWSDK apps are ESM-only — no `require`. _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_
2. **Per-frame logic MUST be an ECS System** — `class X extends createSystem({...})` with
   `update(delta, time)`, registered via `world.registerSystem(X)`. `world.onUpdate`,
   `world.ecs.registerSystem`, `renderer.setAnimationLoop`, and raw `requestAnimationFrame`
   do **not** tick the loop and silently ship a dead game. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
3. **`World` is fully typed — never `(world as any)`.** `world.camera`, `world.scene`,
   `world.renderer`, `world.input`, `world.player`, `world.registerSystem` all exist. A TS error
   here is a real bug, not a framework quirk. _[Phase 1 → 02-world-and-loop.md]_
4. **PanelUI loads async, is uikit (not DOM), and needs relative config paths.** Wire handlers
   in the query `'qualify'` event (not `setTimeout`); mutate with `setProperties` (not
   `textContent`/`onclick`); author `config: './ui/x.json'` relative (root-absolute breaks deploy: GitHub 404s, and the Space daemon serves only `assets/`).
   _[Phase 3 author → 04-spatial-ui.md · Phase 5 bites → 06-build-and-deploy.md]_
5. **`tsc --noEmit` + a rendered screenshot is the real verification gate.** `vite build` does
   not type-check; a green build is **not** verification. Re-run the gate every milestone.
   _[Phase 4 → 05-verify-runtime.md]_
6. **Use the real subsystem APIs, not hand-rolled substitutes.** Physics needs `PhysicsShape` +
   `PhysicsBody` (don't write an integrator); audio uses `AudioSource` / `AudioUtils` (not raw
   Web Audio). _[Phase 2 → 03-subsystems.md]_

When unsure of any API, confirm it with `npx iwsdk reference api|systems|components` (warm it up
first) rather than casting to `any`.

## Phase highlights

### Phase 0 — Scaffold & First Render → `references/01-scaffold-and-render.md`

- Check `node -v` ≥ 20.19 **before** scaffolding (Vite 7 throws `crypto.hash is not a function`
  on Node 18; `dev:runtime` spawns a child `vite` that reverts to the default Node — make 20/22
  the persistent default, don't drop it in `/tmp`).
- Scaffold **fresh** with `npx @iwsdk/create@latest … --no-metaspatial`; infer flags from the
  request. Never clone-and-rename a prior game (drags stale assets/config/anti-patterns).
- `npm run dev` == `npx iwsdk dev up`; read `localUrl` (`https://127.0.0.1:8081/`) from
  `dev status`. SwiftShader auto-detects in 0.4.x — no `node_modules` patching.
- **Warm up the reference CLI now:** `npx iwsdk reference warmup` (so API lookups work later).
- Behind a proxy / on Node-18 / on a Playwright `chromium_headless_shell-1208` mismatch → load
  the reference.

### Phase 1 — World & the Game Loop → `references/02-world-and-loop.md`

- `await World.create(container, {...})`; options are typed (don't cast). Rules **2 and 3** live
  here — this is the costly correctness fork.
- After `World.create`, register systems: `world.registerSystem(GameSystem)`. The loop is
  already running; never start your own.

### Phase 2 — Gameplay Subsystems → `references/03-subsystems.md`

- Three imports (rule 1), physics components (rule 6), `AudioSource`/`AudioUtils` (rule 6),
  XR controller + keyboard input, coordinates. One file, jump via its `## Contents`.
- Input gamepad APIs take **string ids** (`InputComponent.Trigger`) and `getAxesValues` returns
  `{x,y}` — a number id or array index silently returns `undefined`.

### Phase 3 — Spatial UI (PanelUI / uikitml) → `references/04-spatial-ui.md`

- Author `ui/*.uikitml`; the `compileUIKit()` vite plugin emits `public/ui/*.json` (no CLI to
  compile). Reference the **compiled** path, **relative** (rule 4).
- Loads async → wire in the `'qualify'` event; elements are uikit → `setProperties`. ASCII-only
  glyphs; no multi-value `padding`/`margin` shorthand.

### Phase 4 — Verify in the Live Runtime → `references/05-verify-runtime.md`

- Rule 5. `npx tsc --noEmit` → `dev up` → `browser screenshot` → `browser logs` (zero errors) →
  `ecs systems` (your systems are registered = the loop is real). Re-run every milestone.
- `ecs pause`/`step` to freeze a play frame for a stable screenshot.

### Phase 5 — Build & Deploy → `references/06-build-and-deploy.md`

- `npm run build`, then **deploy as a Hatch Space by default**: `create_space` (registers it) →
  rewrite `index.html` (both `data-generated-space-root=""` + `data-generated-space-bootstrap=""`
  markers, drop the SDK bundle) → move every asset under `assets/` → patch runtime paths to
  `./assets/…` → `share share-space --slug <slug>`. Rebuild before publishing (stale-artifact trap).
- **GitHub Pages is opt-in** — only when the user explicitly asks (or needs VM-independent
  hosting, since a `local` Space needs the VM running): `npm run build && npx gh-pages -d dist`.
- The #1 deploy break: the Space daemon serves **only `assets/`**, so any runtime-fetched path
  (PanelUI `config`, gltf/audio/textures) 401s unless it resolves under `./assets/` — and the same
  root-absolute `config: '/ui/…'` 404s on a GitHub subpath. Author configs relative.

### Phase 6 — Iterate → `references/07-iterate.md`

- Re-run the full Phase 4 gate **every** round against _this_ build; never reuse a prior round's
  counts/claims. Recover an unresponsive runtime; re-sync adapters after dependency changes.

## When to load references (route by what you're doing)

| If you're… / you hit…                                                  | Load                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| "nothing is ticking / `ecs systems` is empty / dead loop"              | `references/02-world-and-loop.md`   |
| "a panel won't appear or bind"                                         | `references/04-spatial-ui.md`       |
| "401/404 on panels or assets **after deploy**" (Space or Pages)        | `references/06-build-and-deploy.md` |
| "build passed — is it done?"                                           | `references/05-verify-runtime.md`   |
| confirming a real member/signature (instead of casting to `any`)       | `references/api-quick-reference.md` |
| any `iwsdk` command, warmup recovery, or "does this subcommand exist?" | `references/cli-reference.md`       |
| a raw error string you can't place                                     | `references/troubleshooting.md`     |

Run the bundled gates with `bash scripts/preflight-checks.sh` (non-ASCII glyphs, Yoga shorthand,
root-absolute UI configs, CJS idioms, uikitml→json coverage) before deploying.

## Top friction points (most-wasteful mistakes, by phase)

1. Shipping a **no-op game loop** via raw rAF / `world.onUpdate` / `world.ecs.registerSystem` — _P1_.
2. Reinventing **PanelUI readiness** with `setTimeout` instead of the `'qualify'` event — _P3_.
3. **Assets the Space daemon won't serve** (everything fetched at runtime must live under `assets/`) / root-absolute UI config that 404s on a GitHub subpath — _P3/P5_.
4. Treating a green `vite build` as **verification** (it doesn't type-check) — _P4_.
5. Casting `(world as any)` / guessing APIs instead of warming up the **reference CLI** — _all phases_.
6. Building on **Node 18** (Vite 7 `crypto.hash`) or cloning a prior game as a scaffold — _P0_.
